mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust economic expansion, leading to higher expected corporate earnings and positive equity returns. This signal is based on the 3-month rate of change.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a rolling 3-year period, indicating no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year change in CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%, it signals persistent inflation that will force restrictive monetary policy from the central bank, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 exhibits positive average 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of slowing retail sales (RSAFS) and a high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates that monetary policy is successfully dampening consumer demand, which precedes an earnings recession and negatively impacts equity markets.
failure_condition: A strategy based on this signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark on a risk-adjusted basis (Sharpe ratio) over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A strong positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) signals broad-based economic strength, supporting corporate revenues and profitability, leading to positive equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 6-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE), known as the Sahm Rule indicator, is a reliable real-time signal of a recession. This sharp deterioration in the labor market leads to negative revisions in growth and earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises > 0.5pp above its 12-month low
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to post negative median 60-day returns in the 3 instances following a trigger event.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of weakening aggregate demand, as construction is highly sensitive to interest rates and consumer confidence. This slowdown precedes broader economic weakness, negatively affecting equities.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in HOUST and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The absolute level of the Federal Funds rate acts as a valuation anchor. When FEDFUNDS is above the long-term neutral rate estimate (e.g., 2.5%), policy is restrictive, compressing equity multiples and leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is above 2.5%, the average 5-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not statistically different from periods when it is below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is low and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating, it signals a tight labor market that will drive wage growth, compress corporate margins, and prompt hawkish central bank action. This 'Phillips Curve' dynamic is negative for equities.
failure_condition: The hit rate of the signal (correctly predicting negative returns) falls below 55% over a 10-year backtest.
